headlines.title: Democratic battles and incumbents on defense: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries
headlines.description: The fight to shape the future of the Democratic Party is playing out in a slew of key primaries Tuesday, as races in six states set the matchups in crucial November battlegrounds and settle personal and ideological battles across the country

headlines.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

headlines.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation
headlines.description: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

headlines.title: Trump to speak at the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Dinner
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday that he would attend the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in July after the spring event was abruptly cut short when a gunman ran through a security checkpoint.

headlines.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release
headlines.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order that lays the foundation for federal testing of the world's most powerful AI systems before they are publicly released. The testing would rely on voluntary collaboration from America’s leading AI companies, like Anthropic, OpenAI and Google. NBC News' Monica Alba reports.

headlines.title: Trump names Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence
headlines.description: President Trump has named Bill Pulte, the head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, as the acting director of national intelligence after Tulsi Gabbard resigned from the position. NBC News' Gabe Gutierrez reports on the appointment and Pulte's past work in the administration.

headlines.title: Trump-Netanyahu Tensions Flare Over Israel's Strikes on Lebanon
headlines.description: Israel’s intensifying war in Lebanon has become the main sticking point blocking a peace deal with Iran. In a phone conversation with Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu, President Donald Trump reportedly exploded on the call, cursing, calling him “crazy” and accusing the prime minister of “ingratitude.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Massive leadership vacuum weakens U.S. diplomacy in the face of global crises
headlines.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: U.S. plan to open Ebola quarantine center in Kenya faces growing backlash and protests
headlines.description: Plans for Americans exposed to Ebola to be quarantined abroad faced mounting backlash Tuesday, with Kenya’s president defending a proposed 50-bed facility in the country after violent protests

headlines.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release
headlines.description: President Trump signed an order seeking the establishment of a voluntary framework for AI companies to provide the government early access to new models.

headlines.title: Major Russian attack kills at least 22 and leaves Ukraine pleading for help from Trump
headlines.description: Russian air attacks on major Ukrainian cities such as Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv killed at least nine and wounded more than 60 early Tuesday, authorities said, after days of warnings that Moscow was planning a major assault.
